title: Database Design Phases: Logical vs. Physical Designing
lecture_title: Database Management System: Database Design & Schema Architecture
explanation:
Database design organizes data using a model. Before implementation, experienced designers gather requirements to create a blueprint, similar to drawing a house blueprint map before construction. Database designers are highly experienced professionals; newcomers do not design. The designer first analyzes the business domain (e.g. food delivery like Zomato, car reservation like BlaBlaCar, or ticket reservation like Indigo/MMT) to determine which model to fit the data into. Designing consists of two phases:
1. Logical/Conceptual Design: Defines relationships, dependencies, and entities (e.g., ER diagrams) without hardware details.
2. Physical Design: Configures storage media, file mappings, sectors/tracks, RAID, partitioning, backups, security, and replication.

concepts.definition: The organization of data according to a database model, determining what data must be stored and how data elements interrelate.
An experienced professional who gathers client requirements to plan what data must be stored and how elements interrelate.
The phase of database design that deals with defining the conceptual entities, attributes, relationships, and dependencies without hardware details.
The phase of database design specifying the configuration of data on storage media, including partitioning, replication, RAID levels, backups, and security.

examples.sentence: Designing a database structure for applications like Zomato (food delivery) or BlaBlaCar/Indigo/MMT (reservations) by gathering requirements.
examples.working: Gather requirements from client -> analyze type of data (food, car, ticket) -> decide what data to store -> fit data into model (e.g. relational tables).

examples.sentence: Creating an Entity-Relationship (ER) diagram for a university database.
examples.working: Identify entities (Student, Faculty, Hostels, Courses) -> determine relationships (Student enrolls in Course) and cardinalities (one-to-one, one-to-many) -> map dependencies (attributes).

examples.sentence: Partitioning database files and setting up RAID configurations on server storage.
examples.working: Decide where files reside on physical hardware sectors/tracks -> configure redundant backup storage (RAID) -> schedule restore points to prevent data loss.

important_points: Database designers are highly experienced professionals. Newcomers do not perform database designing; they only help in gathering initial requirements.
Always complete logical designing and blueprints before starting coding or database physical implementation.

visual_moments.description: Database design and requirements slide highlighting organization and fit to models.
Logical Designing notes detailing relationships, dependencies, and mapping to DBMS tables.
Physical Design slide showing storage media configuration, RAID, partitioning, and backup schemes.

teacher_quotes: Database design is simply the organization of data according to a database model.
Logical designing simply deals with relationships and dependencies amongst various objects.

title: Data Abstraction Layers and Three-Schema Architecture
explanation:
Introduces Data Abstraction and the Three-Schema Architecture to hide hardware details from users.

It consists of three levels:
1. Physical/Internal Level: Defines physical storage in hardware (tracks, sectors), hidden from users (e.g. YouTube California server locations).
2. Logical/Conceptual Level: Defines what data is stored and relationships (tables, types, schemas), managed by DBAs.
3. External/View Level: Defines how users view data via front-end UIs (e.g. Student vs Chancellor university logins).

The teacher maps this architecture to a Three-Tier Web Application:
- View Level = Front-end UI (Laptops, desktop browsers, or mobile apps using HTML/CSS).
- Conceptual Level = Backend Server (Application code in PHP, Java, C# handling request processing).
- Physical Level = Database Server (Physical data store in MySQL, Oracle, SQL Server).

Front-end requests go to the server, which validates them against the database. This protects the physical database from direct user access.

concepts.definition: The process of hiding unwanted, irrelevant, or low-level technical details from the end user to simplify database interaction.
A standard DBMS framework dividing database views into three layers: View level (External), Conceptual level (Logical), and Internal level (Physical).
An architecture where the client UI (View level) requests data from a middle application server (Conceptual level) which queries the database server (Physical level).

examples.sentence: A user searches for 'Best course for RPSC programmer' on Google.
examples.working: User enters query in Chrome UI (External/View Level) -> Request goes to server (Conceptual Level) -> Server queries database storage (Internal Level) -> returns results to UI without showing table schemas.

examples.sentence: A university portal shows different views to a Student compared to an HOD, Principal, or Chancellor.
examples.working: Student login displays grade table only. Chancellor login displays full database views and faculty salary details (Role-based View Level access).

examples.sentence: Hiding the exact server disk location of YouTube videos from end-users.
examples.working: User Clicks Play -> Extracted stream ID -> Translated block address on server disk -> Abstracted stream to screen.

examples.sentence: A web application (such as Google or Facebook) requesting and fetching data using a Three-Tier Web Architecture.
examples.working: User fills out a form or enters login details on the Front-end UI (View Level) -> Request is sent to the Server (Conceptual Level) running backend code (PHP, Java, C#) -> Server connects to the Database (Physical Level, such as MySQL or Oracle) to verify credentials or query data -> Database processes physical files and returns data -> Server compiles data -> Server sends response back to client UI.

important_points: Data Abstraction acts as an API layer that prevents end-users from directly accessing the database, which is vital for database security.
In Web Applications (Three-Tier), users never have direct access to database files; all requests must flow through the application server layer.

visual_moments.description: Database Abstraction layers definition slide explaining hidden details.
Three-Schema Architecture diagram showing External, Conceptual, and Internal levels.
Descriptions of Physical/Internal Level and Logical/Conceptual Level on screen.
Three-Tier Web Architecture diagram mapping front-end UI, middle-tier server, and database.

teacher_quotes: Data abstraction is a process of hiding unwanted irrelevant details from the end user.
Data abstraction is unified communication between a computer application and database.

title: Logical and Physical Data Independence
explanation:
Details Data Independence enabled by Three-Schema Architecture, ensuring lower-level schema changes do not require rewriting higher-level schemas or applications:
1. Physical Data Independence: Conceptual schema and application programs are unaffected when physical storage changes (e.g., swapping hard disks, migrating to SSDs, changing folder paths, or altering block sizes). SQL queries remain identical.
2. Logical Data Independence: External schemas and application UIs are unaffected when conceptual schema changes (e.g., adding/renaming columns, deleting attributes, or altering data types).
Without data independence, the application would crash frequently, and you would have to write programs from scratch every time schema changes.

concepts.definition: The ability to modify the conceptual schema (tables, columns, types) without changing the external schema or application programs.
The ability to modify the physical schema (storage location, hard disk hardware, block sizes) without changing the conceptual schema or application code.

examples.sentence: A database administrator migrates data files from one physical hard drive to another or moves them due to disk failure.
examples.working: Copy physical data files -> update storage mapping in DBMS -> SQL queries run identically without rewriting code (Physical Data Independence).

examples.sentence: An application update is pushed to WhatsApp, introducing new fields/features in the database table without requiring users to download a rewritten app from scratch.
examples.working: Push server-side update adding table column -> user updates app to see new features -> existing database operations run smoothly without breaking (Logical Data Independence).

important_points: Data independence ensures we do not have to write programs from scratch when schemas change.
Physical changes (like switching hard drives) should be completely transparent to SQL query engines.

visual_moments.description: Data Independence mappings shown on the Three-Schema Architecture diagram.

teacher_quotes: If we change physical level, conceptual level and external level will have no effect.
Data independence ensures we do not have to write program from scratch when schemas change.
